candidate.title: NVIDIA H200 SXM catalog field slice

local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Existing local H200 product-page captures found and reused: raw/crawler/compute-accelerators-nvidia-h200/20260627T102027151096Z-www-nvidia-com-en-us-data-center-h200-7d05aa2873.md, raw/crawler/compute-accelerators-nvidia-h200/20260627T153310569545Z-www-nvidia-com-en-us-data-center-h200-7d05aa2873.md, and raw/crawler/compute-accelerators-nvidia-h200/20260705T041039962650Z-www-nvidia-com-en-us-data-center-h200-a464325a64.md. No new external fetch was needed.
local_checks.wiki_search: Existing curated pages already cited H200 as raw comparison and refresh evidence, and the structured catalog already had nvidia-h200-sxm with 141 GB memory and 4.8 TB/s memory-bandwidth resolved. The gap was the remaining source-visible, schema-supported H200 SXM fields in the official H200 specifications table.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md routes this work to Compute Accelerator Spec Catalog, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, Compute Accelerator Crawl Inventory, and AI Infra Coverage Map; no dedicated H200 page was needed.

duplicate_boundary_checks.result: nvidia-h200-sxm already existed as the canonical H200 SXM SKU with source_refs to nvidia-h200-product-page; no duplicate SKU row was added.
Existing H200 observations covered memory_capacity and memory_bandwidth only; added new observations for memory_type, FP64, FP32, BF16, FP16, FP8, TDP, form_factor, and interconnect.
Existing H200 resolved fields covered 141 GB memory and 4.8 TB/s bandwidth only; added resolved fields pointing to new H200 SXM observations without changing the existing memory rows.

Existing H200 raw comparison row already showed SXM and NVL side by side; the update narrows resolved catalog text to H200 SXM and keeps H200 NVL, TF32, INT8, benchmark, and server/system claims as boundary evidence.
The July 5 H200 refresh was already tracked as refresh evidence for an existing source profile; this task reused it rather than creating a new crawler source or duplicate accelerator row.

Only the hardware-accelerator layer was updated for parent-4 H200 SXM field promotion; blocked KServe, inference-serving postmortem, MLCommons exact-result, and RAG propagation/drift/alert/cost gaps remain unresolved.
Added a separate known_source entry for parent-4 H200 SXM fields without mutating blocked_items or existing blocked coverage_gaps.

Earlier hardware manifests covered Cambricon, Kunlunxin, Biren, Resnics, Corigine, and Yusur field slices or H200 refresh inventory. None had promoted the remaining H200 SXM compute, TDP, form-factor, or interconnect fields into resolved_specs.

gap_reason: The local corpus already contained an official NVIDIA H200 product-page capture whose H200 SXM column exposed schema-supported fields beyond the previously resolved 141 GB memory and 4.8 TB/s memory bandwidth rows.

existing_duplicate_fields.boundary: Already resolved before parent-4; left unchanged to avoid duplicate observations or churn.

schema_boundary.boundary: Not mixed into nvidia-h200-sxm because the source distinguishes H200 SXM and H200 NVL columns with different compute, TDP, form-factor, interconnect, and AI Enterprise values.
Not promoted because spec-fields.yaml has no tf32_tflops field.
Not promoted because spec-fields.yaml models INT8 as int8_tops with TOPS units, while the source row is written as TFLOPS in the local capture.
Not promoted because spec-fields.yaml has only fp64_tflops, and the non-tensor FP64 row already provides 34 TFLOPS for that field.

schema_boundary.source_value: Llama2, GPT-3, and HPC speedup claims
schema_boundary.boundary: Not promoted because benchmark uplift ratios are comparison/performance context, not single-GPU theoretical spec fields.

schema_boundary.source_value: Decoders, Confidential Computing, Multi-Instance GPUs, Server Options, and NVIDIA AI Enterprise rows
schema_boundary.boundary: Not promoted because the current catalog slice targets core single-GPU spec fields and does not have decoder, confidential-computing, MIG, server-option, or software-bundle fields.

schema_boundary.source_value: H800 runtime mentions and Blackwell B200/B300/GB200/GB300 portfolio captures
schema_boundary.boundary: Not promoted because they are separate runtime or portfolio evidence and do not provide H200 SXM product-spec fields.

non_promoted_boundaries: No new H200 NVL SKU row was created because this task is bounded to the existing H200 SXM identity.
No H200 benchmark speedup, server option, HGX/MGX aggregate, MIG, confidential-computing, decoder, or NVIDIA AI Enterprise software claim was promoted into resolved fields.

No H800 runtime issue, Blackwell portfolio page, KServe, inference-serving postmortem, MLCommons exact-result, RAG propagation/drift/alert/cost, Corigine, Yusur, Resnics, Biren, Cambricon, or Kunlunxin row was modified.
